title: Savory Spicy Korean Tempeh Tagine
description: A plant-based, Korean-inspired dish featuring tempeh, desiccated coconut, and fermented ingredients, cooked in a traditional clay tagine vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and Gochugaru.
Add the tempeh to the bowl and toss to coat with the sauce.
Heat the sesame oil in the bottom of a clay tagine vessel over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic to the tagine and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the coated tempeh to the tagine and cook for 2 minutes, until browned on all sides.
Add the desiccated coconut to the tagine and stir to combine.
Cover the tagine with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20 minutes, until the tempeh is cooked through and the sauce is jammy.
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve hot.
